items.prompt: The vector (cross) product a x b is:
items.answer: a vector of magnitude |a||b| sin(theta) perpendicular to both a and b

expected.correct: a vector of magnitude |a||b| sin(theta) perpendicular to both a and b
options: a vector of magnitude |a||b| sin(theta) perpendicular to both a and b
always equal to b x a
a vector in the plane of a and b

items.prompt: Contrast the scalar product and the vector product of two vectors: state what kind of object each produces and one key property of the cross product.

expected.rubric: The scalar (dot) product a.b = |a||b| cos(theta) is a scalar (a number). The vector (cross) product a x b = |a||b| sin(theta) n-hat is a vector, perpendicular to both a and b (n-hat a unit normal in a right-handed system). A key property of the cross product is that it is not commutative: a x b = -(b x a) (its magnitude also equals the area of the parallelogram with sides a and b).
markPoints: Dot product is a scalar = |a||b| cos(theta)
Cross product is a vector = |a||b| sin(theta) n-hat, perpendicular to both
Cross product is anticommutative: a x b = -(b x a)
